folio.label: 141r
folio.l: Go brynge hire fortú , and put hire in oure warde
The cherl shal han his thral , this I awarde
¢ And whan this wortúy knygút , Virginius
Thurgú sentence , of this Iustice Apius
Muste by force , his deere dougúter ©iuen
Vn to the Iustice , in lecherye to lyuen
He gotú hi~ hom , and sette hi~ in his halle
And leet a noon , his deere dougúter calle
And witú a face deed , as asshen colde
Vp on hire humble face , he gan biholde
Witú fadres pitee , stikynge thurgú his herte
Al wolde he , from his purpos nat conuäte
¢ Dougúter q¶ he , Virginia by thy name
There ben two weyes , either detú or shame
That thow must suffre , allas that I was bore
For neuäe thow deseruedest , wherfore
To dyen witú a swerd , or witú a knyf
O deere dougúter , endere of my lyf
Whicú I haue fostred vp , witú swicú plesaunce
That thow were neuäe , out of myn remembraunce
O dougúter , whicú that art , my laste woo
And in my lif , my laste ioye also
O gemme of chastitee , in pacience
Take thow thy detú , for this is my sentence
For loue , and nat for hate , thow must be ded
My pitous hand , moot smyten of thyn hed
Allas , that euäe Apius the say
Thus hatú he falsly , Iuged the to day
And told hire al the cas , as ©e bifore
Han herd , nat nedetú it , to telle it more
¢ O mercy deere fader , quod this mayde
And witú that word , she bothe hir armes leyde
A boute his nekke , as she was wont to doo
The teeris borst out , of hire eigúen two
And seide , goode fader , shal I dye
Is there no grace , is æäeæäe no remedye
¢ No certes , deere dougúter myn q¶ he
¢ Thanne ©if me leiser , fader myn q¶ she
My detú for to compleyne , a litel space
For pardee , Iepte ©af his dougúter grace
For to compleyne , er he hir slow , allas
And god it woot , no thyngö was hir trespas
But for she ran , hir fader first to se
To welcome hi~ , witú gret solempnytee
